paper_overview.citation: Mandal, P., Upadhyay, R. & Hasan, A. Seasonal and spatial variation of Yamuna River water quality in Delhi, India. Environ Monit Assess 169, 629–642 (2010). https://doi.org/10.1007/s10661-009-1265-2

data_sources.location: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), New Delhi, India
data_sources.description: Routine monitoring data of Yamuna River water quality

sites.description: Palla, about 15 km upstream from Wazirabad barrage near cremation ground. This location reflects the water quality before receiving wastewater discharges from Delhi and raw water quality for Delhi's water supply. It also reflects the impact of domestic and industrial discharges from Sonepat District of Haryana.
sites.source_quote: Palla: About 15 km upstream from Wazirabad barrage near cremation ground. Sampling at this location reflects the water quality before receiving the wastewater discharges from Delhi and raw water quality for Delhi's water supply. Water quality at this location also reflects the impact of domestic and industrial discharges from Sonepat District of Haryana.

sites.description: Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream (NMS), 13 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage (near Sarai Kale Khan bus stand) at Delhi-Ghaziabad (Noida) Road Bridge on Yamuna River. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of wastewater discharge. It is located within a few meters of Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream.
sites.source_quote: Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream and Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream: 13 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage (near Sarai Kale Khan bus stand) at Delhi-Ghaziabad (Noida) Road Bridge on Yamuna River. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of wastewater discharge. Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream and Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream were within few meters. These two sampling locations have been chosen for accuracy of pollution load at selected stretch.

sites.description: Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream (NQS), 13 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage (near Sarai Kale Khan bus stand) at Delhi-Ghaziabad (Noida) Road Bridge on Yamuna River. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of wastewater discharge. It is located within a few meters of Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream.
sites.source_quote: Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream and Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream: 13 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage (near Sarai Kale Khan bus stand) at Delhi-Ghaziabad (Noida) Road Bridge on Yamuna River. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of wastewater discharge. Nizamuddin Bridge Midstream and Nizamuddin Bridge Quarter Stream were within few meters. These two sampling locations have been chosen for accuracy of pollution load at selected stretch.

sites.description: Agra Canal Midstream (ACMS), 24 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage and East of Delhi-Agra national highway near Madanpur Khaddar village. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of discharge of treated and partially treated effluents from Okhla Sewage treatment Plant. It is located within a few meters of Agra Canal Quarter Stream.
sites.source_quote: Agra Canal Midstream and Agra Canal Quarter Stream: 24 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage and East of Delhi-Agra national highway near Madanpur Khaddar village. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of discharge of treated and partially treated effluents from Okhla Sewage treatment Plant. Agra Canal Midstream and Agra Canal Quarter Stream were within few meters. These two sampling locations have been chosen for accuracy of pollution load at selected stretch.

sites.description: Agra Canal Quarter Stream (ACQS), 24 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage and East of Delhi-Agra national highway near Madanpur Khaddar village. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of discharge of treated and partially treated effluents from Okhla Sewage treatment Plant. It is located within a few meters of Agra Canal Midstream.
sites.source_quote: Agra Canal Midstream and Agra Canal Quarter Stream: 24 km downstream from Wazirabad barrage and East of Delhi-Agra national highway near Madanpur Khaddar village. The water quality at this location reflects the impact of discharge of treated and partially treated effluents from Okhla Sewage treatment Plant. Agra Canal Midstream and Agra Canal Quarter Stream were within few meters. These two sampling locations have been chosen for accuracy of pollution load at selected stretch.

extraction_notes: No explicit latitude and longitude coordinates were provided for the sampling sites. The matrix is assumed to be 'water' and sample type 'river water' based on the study context of Yamuna River water quality monitoring.
